diagrams.title: CFG to PDA Conversion Model
diagrams.description: This diagram shows the standard construction that converts a Context-Free Grammar into an equivalent Pushdown Automaton. It displays the state machine structure with start state, intermediate states, and final accepting state, along with the stack operations (push/pop) that simulate grammar productions.
diagrams.purpose: Explains how a Pushdown Automaton can be constructed to accept exactly the language generated by a Context-Free Grammar, demonstrating the equivalence between CFGs and PDAs.

diagrams.title: CFG-PDA Equivalence Relationship
diagrams.description: This diagram illustrates the conceptual relationship between Context-Free Grammars and Pushdown Automata, showing that both formalisms generate exactly the class of Context-Free Languages. It shows CFG on one side, PDA on the other, and the set of Context-Free Languages in the middle with bidirectional arrows indicating equivalence.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the fundamental theorem that CFGs and PDAs are equivalent in expressive power - they both recognize exactly the context-free languages.

diagrams.title: Stack-Based Derivation Simulation
diagrams.description: This diagram shows how a PDA uses its stack to simulate leftmost or rightmost derivations of a CFG. It displays step-by-step how terminals are matched against input while non-terminals are replaced using stack operations, making the connection between grammatical derivations and automaton computations visible.
diagrams.purpose: Helps students understand the operational connection between grammar rules and stack operations - how pushing non-terminals and popping terminals simulates the derivation process.
